Goldman Sachs: Global Oil Inventories Fall to 8-Year Low
headlines.summary: Global oil inventories are crashing and approaching an eight-year low, with the rate of depletion so fast that it exposes the market to further shocks, according to Goldman Sachs. Total oil stocks globally have dropped to about 101 days of expected demand, the lowest level in nea

headlines.title: California Gas Prices Surge Past $6 Amid Oil Supply Crunch
headlines.summary: Californian retail fuel prices have hit $6.114 per gallon for the first time since 2023 amid the global supply crunch that has exposed the state’s vulnerabilities, notably its dependence on foreign oil supply. California sources close to a third of the crude oil it consumes from

headlines.title: Chevron CEO Warns of Emerging Physical Shortages in Crude Oil
headlines.summary: Signs of physical shortages in crude oil are emerging, the chief executive of Chevron warned this week, noting that this will first hit Asian economic growth. “We will start to see physical shortages,” Mike Wirth said at an event, as quoted by Reuters. “Demand needs to move to me

headlines.title: Big Oil Resists Push To Prioritize Output Growth
headlines.summary: It is profit season again, and Big Oil is raking it in, both in Europe and in the U.S., as soaring oil and gas prices boost earnings. What they are not boosting, however, is production. Big Oil is signaling it has no plans to rearrange its priorities. Higher oil prices drove the
